what_it_does: Implements the Layouts drawer for the gallery runtime: it registers a module with DNA.gallery, loads layout archetypes from layouts.json via DNA.api.layouts, renders a scaled preview stage for a selected archetype/canvas/density, supports a narrow-canvas reflow, exports the JSON, and exposes window.toggleLayouts and window.lyRender shims for existing call sites.
observations: Registers itself through DNA.gallery.register('drawer-layouts', { init: ... }) and receives a ctx object.
Calls DNA.api.layouts() to fetch LY (the layouts.json record) and builds a button card per archetype.
Uses ctx.gram(), ctx.tok(), ctx.gm, ctx.surfaces(), ctx.warmth(), and DNA.ordinal() to resolve spacing, type, color, and surface values.
Renders preview HTML with heavy use of inline style strings computed from derived measures.
Supports three composition modes: immersive loose placement, horizontal weighted zones, and vertical frame bands; plus a narrow-canvas reflow that restacks slots in written order.
Implements recursive plate slots via lyPlateHTML, which renders a plate-internal archetype with proportions from ctx.gram().proportion.archetypes['plate-internal'].
Highlights exactly one focal slot per frame using the A.focal value scoped as zone.id + '.' + slot.id.
Exports the full LY object as a downloadable JSON blob when the export button is clicked.
Closes other drawers (tkdrawer, gmdrawer) when opening the layouts drawer and closes on Escape.
Contains hard-coded neutral specimen strings and a LY_SPECIMENS array used as placeholder content.
standouts: Generic archetype renderer: no per-layout hard-coded rendering path; the code is driven by the layouts.json schema.
Focal-slot policy: one highlighted slot per frame, scoped by zone.id + slot.id, with no ranking.
Narrow-canvas reflow treated as a policy: slots are restacked in written order rather than preserving the wide layout.
Recursive plate slots that embed a plate-internal archetype and vary the figure color based on focal scope.
Global compatibility shims window.toggleLayouts and window.lyRender preserved for existing HTML onclick usage.
conventions: Follows the DNA.gallery module registration pattern with an init(ctx) lifecycle.
Uses an IIFE to attach to global window and expose legacy global shims.
Uses event delegation for canvas/density button groups and per-card onclick handlers.
Depends on injected ctx and DNA globals rather than importing them.
Uses inline style attributes for layout geometry instead of CSS classes.
Hard-codes preview viewport sizes (360x640 narrow, 1280x720 wide) and several slot-type heights in the reflow path.
concerns: Large inline style strings are hard to maintain and may complicate theming or debugging.
Hard-coded pixel dimensions and magic numbers for narrow reflow heights reduce adaptability.
Relies on several external APIs (DNA.api.layouts, ctx.gram, ctx.gm, ctx.tok, DNA.ordinal) that are not defined or documented in this file.
Uses innerHTML with content derived from layouts.json data without visible sanitization.
LY_SPECIMENS and placeholder text are fixed English strings, not localized or configurable.
Event listeners (keydown, resize, onclick) are registered during init with no visible teardown path.
loadLayouts is called without await, so the drawer may open before data is available.
notes: A schema-driven layout preview drawer that translates declarative archetypes into scaled HTML using grammar-derived measures, with backward-compatible global shims.
questions: What is the exact schema of layouts.json, especially archetype fields like frame, bands, zones, split, focal, reflow, and evidence?
Where are DNA.api.layouts, ctx.gram, ctx.gm, ctx.tok, ctx.surfaces, ctx.warmth, and DNA.ordinal implemented and documented?
Which CSS classes (lydrawer, ly-card, ly-focal, ly-stage, etc.) are defined and where?
How is the focal slot chosen in the JSON, and can there be zero or multiple focal slots?
What consumes the exported layouts.json, and is it meant to round-trip back into the system?
How does the gallery core handle module lifecycle, re-init, and event cleanup?
Are the hard-coded specimen strings and LY_SPECIMENS intended to be replaced by real content in production?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file implements a generic, schema-driven layout drawer with the stated behavior-preserving shims and focal/reflow policies.
apparent_themes: Schema-driven rendering: structure lives in JSON, presentation in code.
Machine-consumable records: archetypes are meant to be generic and resolved at runtime.
Signal economy: exactly one focal slot per frame.
Reflow as policy: responsive behavior is a declared transformation, not pixel tweaking.
Grammar-derived design: spacing, radius, and type sizes come from a shared grammar system.
Backward compatibility: legacy global function shims are preserved.
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a reusable layout gallery that visualizes any layout archetype from a shared JSON schema using runtime-resolved design tokens, while keeping existing HTML onclick integrations working.
novelty: The combination of a fully generic archetype renderer, recursive plate-internal composition, focal-slot scoping without ranking, and a narrow-canvas reflow that reorders slots by written order rather than scaling the wide layout.
contribution: A layout preview renderer that consumes declarative archetype records, produces scaled HTML previews across wide and narrow viewports, supports focal highlighting and reflow policies, and provides export and global-shim affordances for integration.
summary_for_embedding: A gallery drawer module that loads layout archetypes from layouts.json and renders generic, grammar-token-driven, scaled HTML previews with focal highlighting, narrow reflow, and export/shim affordances.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a runtime preview surface for layout archetypes that remains compatible with existing global onclick hooks.

symbols.description: A tiny wrapper around document.getElementById used throughout the drawer to fetch elements such as the drawer, stage, button groups, and readout.

symbols.does: Fetches layouts.json and builds the archetype selector cards.
symbols.description: Calls DNA.api.layouts(), clears the ly-cards container, creates a button per archetype with active-state toggling, and triggers lyRender when a different archetype is selected.

symbols.does: Opens or closes the layouts drawer.
symbols.description: Toggles the lydrawer open class, updates the lybtn active state, closes the tokens and grammar drawers if open, and re-renders the preview when opening; also bound to the Escape key.

symbols.does: Returns placeholder HTML for a single slot based on its accepted type.
symbols.description: Generates neutral specimen markup for slot types such as heading, figure, list, image, diagram, pills, person, etc., using derived measures and colors from LYC; delegates plate slots to lyPlateHTML.

symbols.does: Renders a plate slot as a self-contained internal frame.
symbols.description: Uses the plate-internal proportion from the grammar, cycles through LY_SPECIMENS, and colors the figure gold only when the plate is the focal slot within the current frame.

symbols.does: Renders the contents of a composition zone.
symbols.description: Handles grouped grid splits with spanning cells, horizontal splits with movement/connector slots, centered title bands, and single-slot bands, wrapping each slot in focal classes and surface backgrounds.

symbols.does: Main render routine that builds the preview stage and readout.
symbols.description: Resolves the selected archetype, computes derived measures from the grammar and warmth tokens, chooses between narrow reflow, immersive loose placement, horizontal weighted zones, or vertical frame bands, injects the preview HTML, scales it to fit the stage, and updates the textual readout and captions.

symbols.does: Scales the preview inner element to fit the visible stage.
symbols.description: Calculates the stage size and a scale factor based on the current canvas mode, then applies a CSS transform and positions the inner preview element.
